req.title: Open savings account (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct behaviours: (1) happy-path account creation with tier assignment, (2) rejection of an initial deposit below $100, (3) the exact boundary value of $100 (minimum allowed), and (4) idempotency on duplicate submission. EP drives the happy-path and rejection tests; BVA targets the $100 boundary; STC models the idempotency state machine (pending→created→duplicate-same-result); NEG covers the sub-minimum deposit. A fifth DT test cross-checks the verified/unverified × valid/invalid-balance decision grid. Five tests satisfy the minimum-three requirement while providing broad coverage of all stated error pathways and expected outcomes.

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit_positive
actions: POST /accounts/savings with body {customer_id, initial_deposit: 500.00} and header Idempotency-Key: <uuid>
expected: response body contains interest_tier assigned automatically
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_valid_deposit_positive(api_client, verified_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 500.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    response = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 201
    body = response.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert "interest_tier" in body
    assert body["interest_tier"] is not None
    assert float(body["initial_balance"]) == 500.00

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_deposit_negative
actions: POST /accounts/savings with body {customer_id, initial_deposit: 99.99} and unique Idempotency-Key
expected: response body contains error code indicating minimum balance violation
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_below_minimum_deposit_negative(api_client, verified_customer):
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 99.99
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": str(uuid.uuid4())}

    response = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert response.status_code == 422
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("detail", ""))).lower()
    assert "minimum" in error_text or "balance" in error_text or "100" in error_text

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission
preconditions: one Idempotency-Key reused for both requests (state: created → duplicate)
expected: First call: HTTP 201 Created with account_id and interest_tier
Second call: HTTP 200 or 201 with identical account_id and interest_tier as first response
trace: R-FIN-077: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key returns original result
tests.executable:
import uuid
import pytest

def test_open_savings_account_idempotent_duplicate_submission(api_client, verified_customer):
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "customer_id": verified_customer["id"],
        "initial_deposit": 200.00
    }
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    first = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert first.status_code in (200, 201)
    first_body = first.json()

    second = api_client.post("/accounts/savings", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert second.status_code in (200, 201)
    second_body = second.json()

    assert second_body["account_id"] == first_body["account_id"]
    assert second_body["interest_tier"] == first_body["interest_tier"]

tests.name: test_open_savings_account_decision_table_unverified_customer
actions: POST /accounts/savings with unverified customer_id and initial_deposit: 500.00
expected: response body indicates customer is not verified or not eligible
